data.title: Tennessee bans encrypted ATMs across the board as anti-fraud regulations are tightened.
data.description: Tennessee Governor Bill Lee has signed a bill banning cryptocurrency ATMs statewide, making Tennessee the second state after Indiana to implement a complete ban. The bill (HB 2505) has officially taken effect and will be enforced on July 1st. Under the regulations, installing or operating a "virtual currency self-service terminal" (i.e., a Bitcoin ATM) will be considered a Class A misdemeanor, punishable by up to one year in jail and a $2,500 fine. Merchants who allow such equipment to operate will also face legal liability. Currently, most states in the US have strengthened regulations through licensing systems and transaction limits, but complete bans remain rare. Data shows that since 2026, 30 states have proposed related legislation, with 20 of them having passed laws, demonstrating a continued tightening of controls on the risks of cryptocurrency ATM fraud.

data.title: White House: Investigation into Powell is ongoing
data.description: The White House: The investigation into Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is ongoing. The case against the Fed is not necessarily going to be dropped.

data.title: Iranian Officials Refute Western Media Reports on Diplomatic Engagements
data.description: Tasnim News Agency has dismissed claims from Western media suggesting that Parliament Speaker Qalibaf would not accompany Foreign Minister Araghchi. According to NS3.AI, Tasnim further clarified that Iranian officials have not engaged in negotiations with the U.S. in Islamabad.

data.title: Brazil Bans Prediction Markets on Elections and Sports Events
data.description: Brazil has implemented a ban on prediction markets related to elections and sporting events, aiming to tighten regulations on this rapidly growing practice. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the global scrutiny these markets are facing. The decision reflects Brazil's efforts to address concerns surrounding the influence and integrity of prediction markets, which have gained popularity worldwide. This move is part of a broader initiative to regulate activities that could impact public perception and decision-making processes. The ban is expected to have significant implications for the industry, as Brazil joins other countries in reevaluating the role of prediction markets in society.

data.title: Solana's MACD Indicator Signals Potential Price Surge
data.description:
Solana's (SOL) moving average convergence divergence (MACD) indicator has issued a 'buy' signal on its weekly chart, historically a precursor to significant price rallies. According to Cointelegraph, this signal has previously led to Solana price increases ranging from 100% to 860%. The MACD, a widely used momentum indicator in technical analysis, helps traders assess the strength, direction, and duration of a trend in an asset's price. The last occurrence of this 'buy' signal was in May 2025, which preceded a 100% rally, pushing the price above $250 by September 18, 2025, from around $125. In previous years, SOL/USD gains were 860% in 2023 and 617% in 2021.

The relative strength index (RSI) is also showing signs of recovery, having climbed to 35 from 25 in mid-February. This level previously marked Solana's bear market bottom in 2022, which was followed by a 2,500% surge to $210 in March 2024, from below $10 in December 2022. Analyst Tyler Hill noted that Solana's RSI has reached sub-35 levels for the first time in 1,200 days, indicating a potential market bottom similar to past cycles. Fellow analyst Sixtysecondalpha highlighted that the MACD cross, combined with the RSI's bullish divergence, suggests Solana's price is poised for its most significant move in two years. Other analysts believe Solana's market setup indicates a macro bottom, paving the way for a continued recovery to new highs.

Solana's price action has formed a symmetrical triangle on the daily chart, with a breakout target of approximately $130, representing a potential increase of over 50.5% from current levels. The pattern will resolve if the SOL/USD pair breaks above the triangle's resistance line at $90. However, resistance from the $90-$96 zone, where the 100-day moving averages converge, could limit the breakout. Solana's cost basis distribution data reveals a large supply overhang above the spot price, with investors holding around 9.9 million SOL at an average cost of $90-$92, creating a potential resistance zone. This concentration suggests many investors may sell at break-even, potentially stalling Solana's rally. As reported by Cointelegraph, Solana's immediate resistance is at $91, which bulls need to flip into support to confirm a trend change.

data.title: U.S. Freezes $344 Million in Cryptocurrency Linked to Iran
data.description: U.S. officials announced the freezing of approximately $344 million in cryptocurrency connected to Iran. According to NS3.AI, Tether played a role in freezing wallet addresses associated with these funds. The assets were reportedly linked on-chain to Iranian entities, as part of broader efforts to intensify economic pressure on Tehran.

data.title: White House Prepares for Possible Vice Presidential Trip to Pakistan
data.description: The White House has announced that Vice President Vance is on standby for a potential visit to Pakistan. According to NS3.AI, officials have stated that they are prepared to depart immediately if the situation requires.

data.title: First Criminal Insider Trading Case in U.S. Prediction Market Involves Master Sgt. Van Dyke
data.description: The U.S. Justice Department has charged Master Sgt. Gannon Ken Van Dyke with insider trading, marking the first such case involving a prediction market participant. According to NS3.AI, prosecutors allege that Van Dyke turned approximately $33,034 in Polymarket bets into about $409,881 by using classified information related to the January raid that led to the capture of Nicolás Maduro. The indictment details that Van Dyke placed 13 YES bets across four Venezuela-related contracts between December 27, 2025, and January 3, 2026. He now faces five federal counts in this unprecedented case.

data.title: Spark Protocol Releases 2026 Q1 Financial Report
data.description: Spark Protocol has announced its financial results for the first quarter of 2026. According to Odaily, the report reveals a total protocol return of $31.5 million, with a net protocol return of $6.91 million and a net protocol surplus of $3.46 million. The current treasury funds of Spark Protocol stand at $46.1 million, and a token buyback of $986,000 in SPK has been completed. The narrowing of lending spreads and increased demand for savings have led to distribution surpassing Spark's liquidity layer as the primary driver of returns.

data.title: Crypto Market Experiences $189 Million in Liquidations Over 24 Hours
data.description:
In the past 24 hours, the cryptocurrency market saw liquidations totaling $189 million, according to Coinglass data. ChainCatcher reports that long positions accounted for $104 million of these liquidations, while short positions made up $85.38 million.

Bitcoin long positions experienced liquidations amounting to $30.45 million, and short positions saw $18 million in liquidations. Ethereum long positions faced $29.29 million in liquidations, with short positions seeing $14.17 million.

Additionally, a total of 85,173 traders were liquidated globally during this period. The largest single liquidation occurred on Hyperliquid's BTC-USD pair, valued at $3.58 million.

data.title: X-Energy's IPO Debut Sees Stock Open at $30.11
data.description: X-Energy, a nuclear reactor technology company, saw its shares open at $30.11 on its first day of trading on the U.S. stock market. According to Jin10, the company's initial public offering (IPO) was priced at $23 per share.

data.title: DeFi Faces Significant Losses from Hacks Compared to Traditional Finance
data.description: Decentralized Finance (DeFi) experienced substantial losses due to hacks last year, according to CryptoSlate analyst Liam Wright. According to NS3.AI, Wright highlighted that DeFi lost $2.8 billion on a transaction volume of $46 trillion, resulting in a loss rate 85 times higher than that of traditional finance. In contrast, IBM data revealed that traditional financial institutions incurred losses of $2.6 billion on approximately $35 quadrillion of transaction volume. This comparison underscores the heightened vulnerability of DeFi systems to security breaches compared to their traditional counterparts.

data.title: Spark: Total protocol rewards reached $31.5 million in Q1 2026
data.description: Spark announced on the X platform that its Q1 2026 financial report has been released. The report shows total protocol returns of $31.5 million, net protocol returns of $6.91 million, and net protocol surplus of $3.46 million. The Spark Protocol treasury currently holds $46.1 million, and a $986,000 SPK token buyback has been completed. Due to narrowing lending spreads and increased demand for savings, distribution has surpassed the Spark liquidity layer as the primary driver of returns for the first time.

data.title: Tennessee Implements Statewide Ban on Crypto ATMs
data.description: Tennessee has enacted legislation prohibiting the operation of cryptocurrency ATMs across the state. According to NS3.AI, the law, which comes into effect on July 1, imposes penalties of up to one year in prison and a $2,500 fine for violations. This ban applies to both kiosk operators and businesses hosting these machines. Tennessee follows Indiana as the second U.S. state to implement a complete ban on crypto ATMs.

data.title: GoDark to Launch Dark-Pool Crypto DEX with $100 Million Daily Volume Target
data.description: GoDark is set to introduce a dark-pool cryptocurrency decentralized exchange (DEX) in May, aiming for a daily trading volume of approximately $100 million by the second month. According to NS3.AI, Denis Dariotis stated that GoDark will utilize offchain nodes and zero-knowledge proofs to conceal perpetual orders, enhancing privacy and security for traders.

data.title: Tether Freezes $344 Million in USDT Amid Law Enforcement Alert
data.description: Tether has frozen over $344 million in USDT across two addresses following identification by U.S. law enforcement agencies of fund flows associated with illicit activities. According to NS3.AI, Tether took immediate action to prevent any further transfer of these funds after the addresses were flagged.

data.title: U.S. Special Envoys to Engage in Talks with Iranian Foreign Minister
data.description: U.S. Special Envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner are set to participate in discussions with Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi. According to NS3.AI, the meeting is intended to explore diplomatic avenues rather than finalize any agreements or policy measures. This engagement reflects ongoing efforts to address complex international relations and seek potential resolutions through dialogue.

data.title: Iran to Propose Plan to End Ongoing Conflict
data.description: Iran's foreign minister is preparing to introduce a plan aimed at resolving the ongoing war. According to NS3.AI, the proposal is expected to be presented soon, as reported by Tasnim News Agency. Details of the plan have not yet been disclosed, but it is anticipated to address key issues contributing to the conflict. The international community is closely monitoring the situation, hoping for a peaceful resolution.

data.title: The United States has frozen $344 million in cryptocurrencies related to Iran.
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent stated that the Treasury Department is sanctioning multiple wallets linked to Iran and will track and strike all financial lifelines connected to the regime. Tether previously announced it had assisted the U.S. government in freezing $344 million worth of cryptocurrency in two addresses. Chainalysis data shows that the activity patterns of these wallets are consistent with the on-chain fund flow characteristics of wallets belonging to the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC). U.S. officials stated that blockchain analysis revealed that the funds were transacted through intermediary addresses with wallets linked to the Central Bank of Iran, used to circumvent sanctions and facilitate international trade. Iran's cryptocurrency holdings are projected to reach $7.8 billion by 2025, with the IRGC holding approximately 50% of that amount in the fourth quarter of last year.

data.title: APE briefly broke through $0.183, surging over 77% intraday.
data.description: OKX market data shows that Ape briefly broke through $0.183 and is currently trading at $0.1765, up more than 77% on the day.
